elements.sent-2411-012: The performance of modern wireless communication systems is typically limited by interference.
elements.sent-2411-013: The impact of interference can be even more severe in ultra-reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC) use cases.
elements.sent-2411-014: A powerful tool for managing interference is rate splitting multiple access (RSMA), which encompasses many multiple-access technologies like non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA), spatial division multiple access (SDMA), and broadcasting.
elements.sent-2411-015: Another effective technology to enhance the performance of URLLC systems and mitigate interference is constituted by reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs).
elements.sent-2411-016: This paper develops RSMA schemes for multi-user multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) RIS-aided broadcast channels (BCs) based on finite block length (FBL) coding.
elements.sent-2411-017: We show that RSMA and RISs can substantially improve the spectral efficiency (SE) and energy efficiency (EE) of MIMO RIS-aided URLLC systems.
elements.sent-2411-018: Additionally, the gain of employing RSMA and RISs noticeably increases when the reliability and latency constraints are more stringent.
elements.sent-2411-019: Furthermore, RISs impact RSMA differently, depending on the user load.
elements.sent-2411-020: If the system is underloaded, RISs are able to manage the interference sufficiently well, making the gains of RSMA small.
elements.sent-2411-021: However, when the user load is high, RISs and RSMA become synergetic.
elements.sent-2411-022: The sixth generation of wireless communication systems (6G) has to substantially improve the spectral efficiency (SE), energy efficiency (EE), peak and average data rate, latency, and reliability \cite{wang2023road, gong2022holographic .}
elements.sent-2411-023: To reach these ambitious goals, 6G has to overcome numerous challenges.
elements.sent-2411-024: In particular, due to a shortage in the available spectrum, we may have to operate in regimes where the number of users is significantly higher than the number of available resource blocks, which leads to strong interference.
elements.sent-2411-025: The impact of interference can be even more severe in ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) systems, which are needed to guarantee the extra requirements of 6G applications such as cloud gaming or robotic aided surgery.
elements.sent-2411-026: A powerful technique to handle interference is rate splitting multiple-access (RSMA), which encompasses non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA), spatial division multiple access (SDMA), broadcasting, and multi-casting \cite{mao2022rate .}
elements.sent-2411-027: Another promising technology conceived for managing interference is represented by reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) \cite{wu2021intelligent, di2020smart .}
elements.sent-2411-028: In this paper, we propose RSMA schemes for enhancing the SE and EE of RIS-aided multi-user (MU) multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) URLLC systems.
elements.sent-2411-029: The optimal treatment of interference critically depends on how strong it is.
elements.sent-2411-030: When the interference is weak, treating interference as noise (TIN) is optimal from a generalized degree of freedom (GDoF) or from a sum-rate maximization point of view \cite{geng2015optimality,annapureddy2009gaussian .}
elements.sent-2411-031: By contrast, the optimal approach to deal with strong interference is to decode, remodulate and subtract it \cite{sato1981capacity , which is referred to as successive interference cancellation (SIC).}
elements.sent-2411-032: Unfortunately, the optimal decoding strategy is unknown or is very complex for the operational regimes between these two extreme cases, which include many practical scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-033: In these cases, we may often have to utilize interference-management techniques, which are not necessarily optimal.
elements.sent-2411-034: One such effective interference-management technique is RSMA, which can exploit the benefits of both the TIN and SIC schemes \cite{mao2022rate .}
elements.sent-2411-035: We refer the motivated reader to \cite{mao2022rate for a distinction between the DL and uplink (UL) RSMA.}
elements.sent-2411-036: The common messages are decoded by a group of (or even all) the users, while the private messages are decoded only by the intended user.
elements.sent-2411-037: Moreover, each user first decodes the corresponding common messages, and then, removes them from the received signal to decode its intended private message.
elements.sent-2411-038: Indeed, to decode the private message, each user applies SIC to the common messages and treats the private messages of the other users as noise.
elements.sent-2411-039: There are various RSMA schemes with different formats for the common messages.
elements.sent-2411-040: The most practical RSMA scheme is the 1-layer rate splitting (RS), which utilizes only a single common message that has to be decoded by all the users \cite{joudeh2016sum, soleymani2022rate, zhou2021rate, mao2021rate, dizdar2021rate, soleymani2023rate, li2020rate, flores2021tomlinson, soleymani2023energy .}
elements.sent-2411-041: Indeed, the complexity of RSMA drastically increases when the number of layers grows, which may affect its practicality.
elements.sent-2411-042: This is even more important for URLLC systems, since decoding multiple layers of common messages may increase the processing latency.
elements.sent-2411-043: Therefore, in this paper, we consider only the 1-layer RS scheme.
elements.sent-2411-044: Another technology to manage the interference and improve the coverage is RIS, which has been shown to be promising in enhancing the performance of various networks, including the broadcast channel (BC) \cite{huang2019reconfigurable, wu2019intelligent, soleymani2024energy, soleymani2022noma , $K$-user interference channel (IC) \cite{jiang2021achievable, bafghi2022degrees, xu2023enhanced, liu2024ris, santamaria2023interference, huang2020achievable , multiple-access channel (MAC) \cite{you2021reconfigurable,fotock2023energy , cognitive radio systems \cite{zhang2020intelligent , and URLLC systems \cite{soleymani2023optimization, soleymani2023spectral, soleymani2024optimization .}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-045: This feature enables RISs to operate as an interference-management technology, especially in ICs \cite{jiang2021achievable, bafghi2022degrees, xu2023enhanced, liu2024ris, santamaria2023interference .}
elements.sent-2411-046: For instance, the authors of \cite{jiang2021achievable proposed interference-neutralizing designs for $K$-user single-input, single-output (SISO) ICs, employing RISs.}
elements.sent-2411-047: Moreover, in \cite{santamaria2023interference , an RIS is used for reducing the interference leakage in a $K$-user MIMO IC.}
elements.sent-2411-048: For instance, the superiority of IGS over proper signaling in RIS-aided systems has been established in \cite{fang2023improper, soleymani2022improper, yu2021maximizing .}
elements.sent-2411-049: Moreover, the authors of \cite{soleymani2022noma, liu2023optimization, li2023ris, soleymani2023noma showed that NOMA can improve the performance of various types of multiple-antenna RIS-aided systems.}
elements.sent-2411-050: Additionally, the benefits of RSMA in RIS-aided BCs has been investigated in \cite{soleymani2022rate, katwe2022ratetwc, niu2023active, zhang2023joint .}
elements.sent-2411-051: Specifically, the authors of \cite{soleymani2022rate proposed an optimization framework for 1-layer RS in MIMO RIS-assisted systems and showed that RSMA can enhance the SE and EE of RIS-aided BCs.}
elements.sent-2411-052: The authors of \cite{niu2023active considered the SE and EE tradeoffs in a single-cell multiple-input single-output (MISO) BC with an active RIS and showed that RSMA is capable of enhancing the system performance.}
elements.sent-2411-053: The studies in \cite{soleymani2022rate, katwe2022ratetwc, niu2023active, zhang2023joint on RSMA for RIS-aided systems showed that RSMA and RISs can be mutually beneficial.}
elements.sent-2411-054: However, these papers considered the asymptotic Shannon rate as the performance metric of interest.
elements.sent-2411-055: Specifically, the achievable data rate of FBL coding is lower than the Shannon rate and depends on the channel dispersion, the packet length, and the decoding error probability.
elements.sent-2411-056: Thus, the existing solutions for RSMA in RIS-aided systems based on the Shannon rate cannot be applied to URLLC systems utilizing FBL coding.
elements.sent-2411-057: There are fewer treatises investigating the performance of RSMA in RIS-aided systems relaying on FBL coding, as shown in Table \ref{table-1 .}
elements.sent-2411-058: Furthermore, there is no work studying RSMA in MIMO systems with FBL coding, not even for systems operating without RISs.
elements.sent-2411-059: The authors of \cite{soleymani2023optimization proposed 1-layer RS schemes for MISO systems assisted by non-diagonal RISs and showed that RSMA and RISs can substantially enhance the SE and EE of multi-cell MISO BCs.}
elements.sent-2411-060: The authors of \cite{katwe2022rate showed that 1-layer RS is capable of increasing the global EE of a single-cell MISO BC aided by an RIS.}
elements.sent-2411-061: In \cite{ singh2023rsma, dhok2022rate , it was shown that RSMA can improve the performance of different RIS-assisted SISO systems.}
elements.sent-2411-062: The authors of \cite{pala2023spectral, katwe2024rsma, jorswieck2025urllc showed that RSMA can improve the DL SE of a BC with a multiple-antenna BS serving multiple single-antenna users.}

elements.sent-2411-063: However, \cite{soleymani2024optimization is the only study on resource allocation designed for multi-stream MU-MIMO systems using FBL coding, and there is no literature on RSMA designed for MIMO URLLC systems.}
elements.sent-2411-064: Resource allocations schemes for FBL encoding are much more difficult to handle than the asymptotic Shannon rate and the solutions obtained for the Shannon rate cannot be reused in RSMA URLLC systems.
elements.sent-2411-065: Upon utilizing FBL coding, the achievable data rate depends on the asymptotic Shannon rate, the channel dispersion, the packet length, and the reliability criterion.
elements.sent-2411-066: The channel dispersion is a fractional function of the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR), which makes the optimization of the FBL rate and/or thr EE much more challenging than in the asymptotic case.
elements.sent-2411-067: Optimizing the rate for FBL coding is even more complex in MIMO systems, where the optimization variables are matrices.
elements.sent-2411-068: In \cite{soleymani2024optimization , the authors proposed resource allocation schemes for MU-MIMO RIS-aided URLLC systems when multiple data streams per user are transmitted.}
elements.sent-2411-069: However, the benefits of RISs are reduced when the number of users grows.
elements.sent-2411-070: Indeed, the level of interference increases with the number of users, and an RIS alone may not be capable of mitigating the interference by itself.
elements.sent-2411-071: Thus, in this paper, we aim for integrating RSMA into MIMO RIS-aided URLLC systems.
elements.sent-2411-072: As shown in Table \ref{table-1 , there is no work on RSMA designed for MIMO systems using FBL coding.}
elements.sent-2411-073: Although the authors of \cite{soleymani2023optimization, singh2023rsma,katwe2022rate, dhok2022rate provided valuable insights into the performance of RSMA in RIS-assisted systems, there are several open research questions, especially for MIMO RIS-aided URLLC systems, that require further investigation.}
elements.sent-2411-074: In particular, our goal is to answer the following questions:.
elements.sent-2411-075: We show that RSMA and RISs provide higher gains when the reliability and latency constraints are more demanding.
elements.sent-2411-076: Moreover, RSMA further improves the gains provided by RISs when the user load is high.
elements.sent-2411-077: Furthermore, the benefits of RSMA are more significant than those of RISs if the number of users is higher than the number of transmit antennas (TAs) at the base station (BS).
elements.sent-2411-078: In this study, we develop energy-efficient and spectral-efficient resource allocation schemes for multi-user MIMO URLLC systems by leveraging RISs and RSMA.
elements.sent-2411-079: To the best of our knowledge, this is the first paper to propose RSMA schemes for MU-MIMO systems with FBL coding, where the asymptotic Shannon rate is not a precise metric for characterizing the achievable data rate.
elements.sent-2411-080: We summarize the main contributions made by this paper, as follows:.
elements.sent-2411-081: We show that RSMA substantially increases the SE and EE of MU-MIMO URLLC systems.
elements.sent-2411-082: This is particularly the case for systems with high user load, which are typically interference-limited, and RISs may not be able to fully mitigate the heavy interference.
elements.sent-2411-083: Therefore, RSMA can provide higher gains over TIN in URLLC systems, especially when the number of transmit antennas at the BSs is less than the number of users.
elements.sent-2411-084: However, when the user load is light, the RISs reduce the benefits of RSMA, since they can effectively mitigate the interference in this case.
elements.sent-2411-085: Hence, there is no need to use sophisticated multiple access schemes in these scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-086: Additionally, the benefits provided by RISs become more substantial in systems having a high user load, when we utilize RSMA.
elements.sent-2411-087: Indeed, when the system performance is limited by interference, increasing the transmission power and/or power budget of the transmitters increases the interference level, and thus, it does not noticeably increase the achievable rate and/or EE.
elements.sent-2411-088: However, when we employ RSMA, the interference is appropriately managed by RSMA, which significantly improves the performance at high signal-to-noise ratios (SNR).
elements.sent-2411-089: The paper is structured as follows.
elements.sent-2411-090: Section \ref{sec=ii presents the network scenario, the channel models associated with RISs, and the signal model.}
elements.sent-2411-091: Moreover, Section \ref{sec=ii derives the rate and EE expressions, and formulates the optimization problems of interest.}
elements.sent-2411-092: Section \ref{sec-iii develops the algorithms proposed for SE and EE maximization.}
elements.sent-2411-093: Section \ref{sec-iv provides numerical results, while Section \ref{sec-v summarizes our findings and concludes the paper.}}

elements.sent-2411-334: The main findings of this study are summarized below:.
elements.sent-2411-335: RSMA and RISs substantially enhance both the SE and EE of MU-MIMO URLLC systems.
elements.sent-2411-336: The SE gains of RISs and RSMA are higher than their EE benefits, since the optimal EE usually favors a lower transmit power than the optimal SE.
elements.sent-2411-337: Moreover, the benefits of RSMA and RISs are increasing functions of the BSs transmission power.
elements.sent-2411-338: RISs impact the performance of RSMA differently, depending on the user load.
elements.sent-2411-339: When the number of users is higher than the number of TAs at the BSs, RISs improve the RSMA gains and vice versa.
elements.sent-2411-340: However, when the user load is low, RISs erase the RSMA gains.
elements.sent-2411-341: RISs and RSMA can provide higher gains when the reliability and latency constraints are more demanding.
elements.sent-2411-342: Particularly, the benefits of RSMA and RIS are enhanced when the maximum tolerable packet error rate and packet length are reduced.
elements.sent-2411-343: RSMA provides higher SE and EE gains than RISs when the user load is higher than one.
elements.sent-2411-344: Additionally, in the scenarios considered, RSMA always outperformed SDMA both with and without RISs.

elements.sent-2411-485: Since 2017, he has been listed as a Highly Cited Researcher by the Web of Science.
elements.sent-2411-486: Robert is a Fellow of the Canadian Academy of Engineering, a Fellow of the Engineering Institute of Canada, and a Member of the German National Academy of Science and Engineering.
elements.sent-2411-487: He served as Editor-in-Chief of the IEEE Transactions on Communications, VP Publications of the IEEE Communication Society (ComSoc), ComSoc Member at Large, and ComSoc Treasurer.
elements.sent-2411-488: Currently, he serves as Senior Editor of the Proceedings of the IEEE and as ComSoc President.
elements.sent-2411-489: He coauthored 2000+ contributions at IEEE Xplore and 19 Wiley-IEEE Press monographs.
elements.sent-2411-490: He was bestowed upon the IEEE Eric Sumner Technical Field Award.. \end{IEEEbiography.}
